title: [phase:repair] [family:verification] [provider_hint:codex] Current task: openclaw-tmux replacement. Review the prototype tests and propose the next 4 verification additions needed before canary. Keep it concise. No edits.
summary_text: Current state check: both runtime workflows already consume these secrets (`NLM_AUTH_TOKEN`, `NLM_COOKIES`) via GitHub `secrets.*`, so workflow YAML changes are not the blocker. Minimal file set to finish bootstrap wiring: 1. [scripts/local/sync-gh-secrets-from-env.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/local/sync-gh-secrets-from-env.sh) Add org-secret sync entries: `FUGUE_NOTEBOOKLM_AUTH_TOKEN <- FUGUE_NOTEBOOKLM_AUTH_TOKEN|NLM_AUTH_TOKEN` and `FUGUE_NOTEBOOKLM_COOKIES <- FUGUE_NOTEBOOKLM_COOKIES|NLM_COOKIES`. 2. [scripts/local/bootstrap-prod-secrets.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/local/bootstrap-prod-secrets.sh) Update the “NotebookLM production vars recognized” help text to include those two secret env names so operators actually provide them. 3. [tests/test-notebooklm-runtime-smoke.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/tests/test-notebooklm-runtime-smoke.sh) Add assertions that the sync script includes both new secret mappings (and optionally that both runtime workflows keep `NLM_AUTH_TOKEN`/`NLM_COOKIES` env wiring). Likely test delta: 2-4 new `assert_grep` checks, no new harness needed. Policy note: this is a single-lane review; canary sign-off should still include an independent non-Codex verification lane.
